meta.title: HELIX review: fresh-eyes audit against the governing artifacts

narration: Build cycle finished. Tests pass. Before merging, a different agent does a fresh-eyes review against the same artifacts that govern the work.

prompt: Use HELIX review on the recent OAuth work in this project. Fresh eyes on plan, artifacts, and implementation. Report findings ordered by severity with concrete evidence.

args.args: review the recent OAuth work — fresh eyes on plan, artifacts, and implementation

tool_result: bead-OAUTH-03 in_review · governing §3.2 + §4 · evidence: refresh.ts, refresh_test.ts
§3.2 token rotation · §4 revocation list · §5 audit row
happy path, expiry, rotation, replay; revocation = TODO

narration: Same artifacts the builder worked from. Different agent. Different attention.

assistant: Review findings — bead-OAUTH-03, ordered by severity:

output:
CRITICAL  §4 revocation list never consulted
            refresh.ts:17-31 validates consumed + expiresAt, then mints.
            evidence: security-architecture.md:18-20 ("MUST consult")

  HIGH      Raw refresh token logged on error path
            refresh.ts:42 — console.error(`token=${opaqueRefresh}`, err)
            evidence: bead acceptance ("MUST NOT appear in logs")

  HIGH      No test exercises revocation rejection
            refresh_test.ts:37-38 is a TODO comment, not a test.

  MEDIUM    §4 access-token unwind unimplemented, no recorded scope-out
  MEDIUM    §5 audit row never emitted on any decision path
  MEDIUM    refresh() signature cannot carry §5 audit context (no ctx/IP)

  LOW       Non-atomic rotation: markConsumed before storeRefresh
  LOW       Bead transitioned to in_review with builder-authored BUG comments

assistant: Verdict: do not advance. Two acceptance clauses unmet, governing artifact mandates behavior the code does not implement. Recommend returning bead-OAUTH-03 to in_progress and filing follow-ups for findings 1-6.

narration: Same lever that lets one agent ship in an afternoon lets a second agent audit it before merge. The artifacts mediate both.
